mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate earnings growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC over 60-day horizons, or S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 60 percent of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during post-shock recovery phases indicate capacity utilization improvements that boost short-term equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism shows negative predictive accuracy (Rank IC < -0.02) when applied outside expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings erode real corporate profit margins and trigger expectations of tighter financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL values correlate with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the Rank IC remains statistically indistinguishable from zero over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent, aggregate demand weakness forces earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 65 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5 percent, or the threshold signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or interaction terms fail to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: During inflationary pressure regimes, simultaneous rises in the federal funds rate and consumer prices signal aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint tightening signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during high-inflation periods, or S&P 500 returns remain positive in over 60 percent of triggered instances despite rising rates and prices.
